user_story: As a CMDB user, I want to log in with my username and password so that I receive access and refresh tokens for authenticated API calls.
expected_behavior: Request passes the 'auth' rate-limit bucket and validation against authSchemas._login, then calls AuthService.login(req.body). On success responds 200 with {success:true, data:result} (token payload). On any thrown error responds 401 {success:false, error:'Authentication Failed', message}. No session is stored server-side; tokens are the only credential returned.

user_story: As an authenticated client, I want to exchange a refresh token for a new access token so that my session continues without re-entering credentials.
expected_behavior: Rate-limited via the 'auth' bucket and validated against authSchemas._refreshToken, then calls AuthService.refreshToken(req.body). On success responds 200 {success:true, data:result} with refreshed tokens. On failure responds 401 {success:false, error:'Token Refresh Failed', message} (e.g. invalid or expired refresh token).

user_story: As an authenticated user, I want a logout endpoint so that the app can signal the end of my session.
expected_behavior: Requires a valid bearer token (authMiddleware.authenticate()). Always responds 200 {success:true, message:'Logged out successfully'}. Logout is purely client-side token discard: the handler performs no server-side token invalidation/blacklist (auth.controller.ts:156-164 only notes a blacklist 'could be implemented'), so a stolen token remains valid until it expires.

user_story: As an authenticated user, I want to generate an API key so that I can authenticate programmatic/CLI access without my password.
expected_behavior: Requires authentication and validation against authSchemas._generateApiKey. If req.user._userId is missing responds 401. Otherwise calls AuthService.generateApiKey(userId, req.body) and responds 200 {success:true, data:result, message:'API key generated. Save it securely - it will not be shown again.'}. Generation failure responds 500 {error:'API Key Generation Failed'}. The raw key is returned once only.

user_story: As an authenticated user, I want to list my API keys so that I can review which keys exist and manage them.
expected_behavior: Requires authentication. If req.user._userId is missing responds 401. Otherwise calls AuthService.listApiKeys(userId) and responds 200 {success:true, data:apiKeys} (key metadata only, not the secret). On error responds 500 {error:'Failed to List API Keys'}.

user_story: As an authenticated user, I want to revoke an API key by id so that compromised or unused keys can no longer be used.
expected_behavior: Requires authentication. Responds 401 if req.user._userId is missing, 400 {error:'Bad Request', message:'Key ID is required'} if the :keyId param is absent. Otherwise calls AuthService.revokeApiKey(userId, keyId) and responds 200 {success:true, message:'API key revoked successfully'}. Errors respond 500 {error:'API Key Revocation Failed'}. Note: revocation is not scoped to verify the key belongs to the user beyond passing userId to the service.

user_story: As an authenticated user, I want to fetch my own identity so that the UI can display who I am logged in as and gate features by role.
expected_behavior: Requires authentication. Responds 200 {success:true, data:{userId, username, role}} sourced directly from the decoded token (req.user._userId/_username/_role). No DB lookup; reflects token claims only.

user_story: As an operator, I want a comprehensive health endpoint so that I can see the status of all backing services in one call.
expected_behavior: Calls HealthCheckService.checkHealth(), which probes Neo4j, Postgres, Redis, disk and memory and returns SystemHealth {_status,_version,_uptime,_timestamp,_checks{...}}. HTTP status is mapped by getStatusCode: healthy/degraded -> 200, unhealthy -> 503, unknown -> 500. If the check itself throws, responds 503 with {_status:'unhealthy',_message,_timestamp}.

user_story: As a Kubernetes/orchestrator, I want a readiness probe so that traffic is only routed to the service when it can serve requests.
expected_behavior: Calls HealthCheckService.checkReadiness() and responds 200 when ready or 503 when not, body {_ready, _message, _timestamp}. If the check throws, responds 503 {_ready:false,_message:'Readiness check failed: ...'}.

user_story: As an orchestrator, I want a liveness probe so that the container is restarted only when the process is genuinely unresponsive.
expected_behavior: Calls HealthCheckService.checkLiveness() and responds 200 when alive or 503 otherwise, body {_alive, _message, _timestamp}. On thrown error responds 503 {_alive:false}. NOTE: the actual route segment is '/alive' (health.routes.ts:34), not '/live' as sometimes referenced.

user_story: As a dashboard, I want aggregated CMDB metrics so that I can render totals (CIs, relationships, anomalies, drift, high-risk changes).
expected_behavior: Opens a Neo4j session and runs counts for total CIs, total relationships, 24h anomalies, 24h configuration drift, and 24h high-risk changes; responds 200 with snake_case JSON. active_connectors (5) and connector_success_rate (94.5) are hard-coded mock values (health.controller.ts:131-133). On error responds 500 {error:'Failed to fetch metrics'}. Session is always closed in finally.

user_story: As an operator, I want each dependency's health and latency so that I can pinpoint which service is degraded.
expected_behavior: Performs live checks of Neo4j (RETURN 1) and Redis (set/get round-trip), recording response_time_ms and status 'healthy' or 'down'. PostgreSQL, Discovery Engine and ETL Processor entries are appended as static mock data with fixed response times (health.controller.ts:212-232). Responds 200 with an array of {service,status,last_check,response_time_ms?}. On error responds 500.

user_story: As a dashboard, I want time-series CI and anomaly counts so that I can plot trends over a chosen window.
expected_behavior: Reads ?hours (default 24) and chooses bucket interval/label format (hourly<=24h, every-3-days<=90d, monthly otherwise). Iterates buckets running a Neo4j count of CIs created on/before each timestamp and anomalies detected within the window, returning 200 with [{time,cis,anomalies}]. On error responds 500 {error:'Failed to fetch time-series data'}.

user_story: As a Prometheus scraper, I want a /metrics endpoint in text exposition format so that I can collect application metrics.
expected_behavior: The router fetches getMetricsRegistry(), serializes via getMetrics(), sets the registry Content-Type and sends the metrics text; errors respond 500 {_error:'Failed to collect metrics'}. However this router is never imported or mounted anywhere (no import of metrics/metrics.routes in rest/server.ts or index.ts), so the endpoint is unreachable in the running app.
issue_note: metrics/metrics.routes.ts is never imported or app.use'd (rest/server.ts setupRoutes, lines 77-103); the Prometheus /metrics endpoint is dead/unreachable.

user_story: As a developer, I want an interactive Swagger UI so that I can browse and try the REST API.
expected_behavior: swaggerRoutes is mounted at /api-docs (rest/server.ts:79, no auth). It serves the Swagger UI from the OpenAPI spec loaded at startup from ../../openapi/openapi.yaml, with custom CSS, title, and try-it-out enabled. If the spec fails to load, a minimal fallback document ('API documentation is currently unavailable') is rendered instead of crashing.

user_story: As a tooling integrator, I want the raw OpenAPI spec as JSON so that I can generate clients or import it into other tools.
expected_behavior: Sets Content-Type application/json and sends the in-memory swaggerDocument object (the parsed YAML, or the fallback document if loading failed). Always 200.

user_story: As a tooling integrator, I want the OpenAPI spec as YAML so that I can consume it in YAML-native pipelines.
expected_behavior: Sets Content-Type text/yaml and returns YAML.stringify(swaggerDocument, 10, 2). Always 200; serves the fallback document if the spec failed to load at startup.

user_story: As an operator, I want a docs health summary so that I can confirm the OpenAPI spec loaded and see how many paths/schemas it exposes.
expected_behavior: Responds 200 {status:'ok', openapi_version, api_version, paths_count, schemas_count} computed from the loaded swaggerDocument. When the spec failed to load, counts are 0 and versions reflect the fallback document.

user_story: As an admin, I want current rate-limit metrics so that I can monitor throttling activity across buckets.
expected_behavior: createRateLimitMetricsRoutes builds a router whose GET '/' calls RateLimitMetricsController.getMetrics to return live rate-limit counters. However this factory is never imported or mounted in rest/server.ts, so the endpoint is unreachable. It is also documented as 'Admin only' but no auth middleware is attached in the route definition.
issue_note: createRateLimitMetricsRoutes (rate-limit-metrics.routes.ts:13) is never imported/mounted in rest/server.ts; the /api/v1/metrics/rate-limits route is dead/unreachable.

user_story: As an admin, I want the rate-limit configuration summary so that I can verify which limits/buckets are configured.
expected_behavior: GET '/config' calls RateLimitMetricsController.getConfiguration to return the configured rate-limit buckets/thresholds. Unreachable for the same reason as PLAT-019: the parent router is never mounted, and no auth gate is wired despite the 'Admin only' note.
issue_note: Same unmounted router as PLAT-019 (rate-limit-metrics.routes.ts:31); /config endpoint is dead/unreachable.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want to query CIs with optional type/status/environment/name filters and pagination so that I can browse the inventory.
expected_behavior: Intended: open a Neo4j session, build a WHERE clause from filter, run MATCH (ci:CI) ... ORDER BY created_at DESC SKIP offset LIMIT limit (defaults limit 100, offset 0), map records to CI objects, throw GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure, close session in finally. Defect: the resolver is keyed '_getCIs' but the schema field is 'getCIs', so the resolver never binds to the schema field.
issue_note: Resolver key Query._getCIs (resolvers/index.ts:116) does not match schema field Query.getCIs (typeDefs.ts:185); resolver is unwired so the non-null field resolves null/errors.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want to fetch a single CI by id so that I can view its details.
expected_behavior: Intended: load the CI via the per-request DataLoader (_loaders._ciLoader.load(id)) for batching/caching; return CI or null; throw GraphQLError on failure. Defect: keyed '_getCI' instead of schema field 'getCI', so the resolver is not invoked.
issue_note: Resolver key Query._getCI (resolvers/index.ts:201) does not match schema field Query.getCI (typeDefs.ts:188); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want to search CIs by name or external id with optional filters so that I can find specific items.
expected_behavior: Intended: build a WHERE matching ci.name CONTAINS query OR ci.external_id CONTAINS query plus optional type/status/environment, ORDER BY name LIMIT limit (default 50), map to CI objects. Defect: keyed '_searchCIs' vs schema field 'searchCIs', so it never binds.
issue_note: Resolver key Query._searchCIs (resolvers/index.ts:222) does not match schema field Query.searchCIs (typeDefs.ts:191); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want a CI's relationships in a chosen direction so that I can understand its connections.
expected_behavior: Intended: for direction 'out' use relationshipLoader, 'in' use dependentLoader, 'both' (default) load and concatenate both; throw GraphQLError on failure. Defect: keyed '_getCIRelationships' vs schema field 'getCIRelationships', so it never binds.
issue_note: Resolver key Query._getCIRelationships (resolvers/index.ts:298) does not match schema field Query.getCIRelationships (typeDefs.ts:194); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want the recursive DEPENDS_ON dependencies of a CI so that I can see what it relies on.
expected_behavior: Intended: run MATCH (ci:CI {id})-[:DEPENDS_ON*1..depth]->(dep:CI) RETURN DISTINCT dep (depth default 5), map to CI objects, close session. Note: depth is interpolated directly into the Cypher path range. Defect: keyed '_getCIDependencies' vs schema field 'getCIDependencies', so it never binds.
issue_note: Resolver key Query._getCIDependencies (resolvers/index.ts:331) does not match schema field Query.getCIDependencies (typeDefs.ts:197); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want impact analysis for a CI so that I can see which CIs would be affected if it fails.
expected_behavior: Intended: run MATCH (ci:CI {id})<-[:DEPENDS_ON*1..depth]-(impacted:CI) RETURN DISTINCT impacted, length(path) as distance ORDER BY distance (depth default 5), map to [{ci, distance}]. Defect: keyed '_getImpactAnalysis' vs schema field 'getImpactAnalysis', so it never binds.
issue_note: Resolver key Query._getImpactAnalysis (resolvers/index.ts:379) does not match schema field Query.getImpactAnalysis (typeDefs.ts:200); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want to create a CI so that new managed components are recorded.
expected_behavior: Intended: validateCIInput (id, name, type required else BAD_USER_INPUT), normalize enum values via convertEnumToDbFormat, default status 'active' and discovered_at now, call neo4jClient.createCI, clear the CI loader cache, return the CI. Defect: keyed '_createCI' vs schema field 'createCI', so it never binds.
issue_note: Resolver key Mutation._createCI (resolvers/index.ts:436) does not match schema field Mutation.createCI (typeDefs.ts:208); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want to update a CI's mutable fields so that the record stays accurate.
expected_behavior: Intended: build a partial update from provided name/status/environment/metadata (enums normalized), call neo4jClient.updateCI(id, updates), clear the loader cache, return the updated CI; throw GraphQLError on failure. Defect: keyed '_updateCI' vs schema field 'updateCI', so it never binds.
issue_note: Resolver key Mutation._updateCI (resolvers/index.ts:477) does not match schema field Mutation.updateCI (typeDefs.ts:211); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want to delete a CI so that decommissioned components are removed.
expected_behavior: Intended: run MATCH (ci:CI {id}) DETACH DELETE ci RETURN count, throw NOT_FOUND if nothing deleted, clear loader cache, return true; close session in finally. Defect: keyed '_deleteCI' vs schema field 'deleteCI', so it never binds.
issue_note: Resolver key Mutation._deleteCI (resolvers/index.ts:520) does not match schema field Mutation.deleteCI (typeDefs.ts:214); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want to create a typed relationship between two CIs so that dependencies are modeled.
expected_behavior: Intended: call neo4jClient.createRelationship(fromId, toId, type, properties), clear relationship and dependent loader caches for both CIs, return true; throw GraphQLError on failure. Defect: keyed '_createRelationship' vs schema field 'createRelationship'; additionally the resolver reads args via _args._input._fromId/_toId/_type (underscored) whereas the schema input is CreateRelationshipInput. Resolver never binds.
issue_note: Resolver key Mutation._createRelationship (resolvers/index.ts:567) does not match schema field Mutation.createRelationship (typeDefs.ts:217); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want to delete a relationship between two CIs so that obsolete dependencies are removed.
expected_behavior: Intended: run MATCH (from:CI {fromId})-[r:TYPE]->(to:CI {toId}) DELETE r RETURN count, throw NOT_FOUND if nothing deleted, clear both loader caches, return true; close session in finally. Defect: keyed '_deleteRelationship' vs schema field 'deleteRelationship', so it never binds.
issue_note: Resolver key Mutation._deleteRelationship (resolvers/index.ts:605) does not match schema field Mutation.deleteRelationship (typeDefs.ts:220); resolver unwired.

user_story: As an API consumer, I want the GraphQL API served at /graphql so that I can run CI/connector/analytics queries and mutations.
expected_behavior: createGraphQLServer builds an ApolloServer from typeDefs + merged resolvers and mounts expressMiddleware at /graphql with a per-request context (Neo4j client + fresh DataLoaders). Defects: (1) the running entry point packages/api-server/src/index.ts only starts RestAPIServer and never calls createGraphQLServer/startGraphQLServer, so /graphql is not served by the app (only referenced in README and integration-example.ts); (2) the resolver export object registers types/scalar under wrong keys (_CI, _JSON, _AnalyticsQuery, _ReconciliationQuery, _ReconciliationMutation) that do not exist in the schema, while schema types are CI, JSON, AnalyticsQuery, etc.
issue_note: index.ts never invokes createGraphQLServer (only RestAPIServer.start()), so /graphql is unmounted; resolver export keys _CI/_JSON/_AnalyticsQuery (resolvers/index.ts:734-741) also mismatch schema type names.

user_story: As a user, I want to browse the connector registry with category/search/tags/verifiedOnly filters so that I can discover available connectors.
expected_behavior: Builds a parameterized SELECT against connector_registry_cache with optional category, name/description ILIKE search, tags array overlap (&&), and verified=true filter; orders verified DESC, downloads DESC, name ASC; maps rows to camelCase connector objects (category upper-cased, rating parsed to float). Throws GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure. Resolver key matches schema (spread into Query), so it is correctly wired.

user_story: As a user, I want full registry details for a specific connector type so that I can review versions and metadata before installing.
expected_behavior: Takes connectorType, queries connector_registry_cache for that connector, returns the mapped detail object or null if not found. Throws GraphQLError on query failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want to list installed connectors (optionally filtered by category) so that I can see what is available in this deployment.
expected_behavior: Queries the installed_connectors table with optional category filtering and returns the mapped list of installed connector objects. Throws GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want details of a single installed connector by type so that I can inspect its configuration capabilities.
expected_behavior: Takes connectorType and returns the matching installed connector record or null if not installed. Throws GraphQLError on query failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want to list connector configurations (optionally by connectorType) so that I can manage scheduled syncs.
expected_behavior: Queries connector_configurations with optional connectorType filtering and returns the mapped list of configuration objects (connection/options/resourceConfigs JSON fields). Throws GraphQLError on failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want a single connector configuration by id so that I can view its full settings.
expected_behavior: Takes id and returns the matching connector_configurations row mapped to the configuration object, or null if not found. Throws GraphQLError on failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want to list connector run history (optionally by config) so that I can audit sync executions.
expected_behavior: Queries connector_run_history with optional configId filtering and returns the mapped list of run records. Throws GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want a single connector run by id so that I can inspect its status and details.
expected_behavior: Takes id and returns the matching connector_run_history record mapped to a run object, or null if not found. Throws GraphQLError on failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want aggregate connector statistics so that I can monitor install counts, configurations, 24h runs, and success rate.
expected_behavior: Runs an aggregate query joining installed_connectors, connector_configurations and connector_run_history to compute totalInstalled, totalConfigurations, totalRuns24h and successRate24h, plus a top-10 connectors list (by total runs with per-connector success rate). Returns these as a stats object; throws GraphQLError on failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want to install a connector from the registry so that it becomes available for configuration.
expected_behavior: Declared in schema and resolver-bound, but the body is a stub: it immediately throws GraphQLError 'Not implemented yet' (code NOT_IMPLEMENTED) with a TODO to implement via a ConnectorInstaller service. No install actually occurs.
issue_note: installConnector is an unimplemented stub that always throws NOT_IMPLEMENTED (connector.resolvers.ts:793-796).

user_story: As an operator, I want to update an installed connector to a new version so that I can keep it current.
expected_behavior: Resolver-bound but a stub: always throws GraphQLError 'Not implemented yet' (NOT_IMPLEMENTED) with a TODO for ConnectorInstaller. No update occurs.
issue_note: updateConnector is an unimplemented stub that always throws NOT_IMPLEMENTED (connector.resolvers.ts:806-809).

user_story: As an operator, I want to uninstall a connector so that it is no longer available.
expected_behavior: Resolver-bound but a stub: always throws GraphQLError 'Not implemented yet' (NOT_IMPLEMENTED). No uninstall occurs.
issue_note: uninstallConnector is an unimplemented stub that always throws NOT_IMPLEMENTED (connector.resolvers.ts:819-822).

user_story: As an operator, I want to create a connector configuration so that I can schedule and run syncs for a connector type.
expected_behavior: Inserts a row into connector_configurations with all fields from input (defaults: enabled true, scheduleEnabled false, maxRetries 3, retryDelaySeconds 300, continueOnError false, notificationOnFailure true) plus created_by from context user (or 'system'); connection/options/resourceConfigs are JSON-stringified. Returns the created configuration mapped to camelCase. Throws GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure.

user_story: As an operator, I want to update a connector configuration so that I can change its schedule, connection, or options.
expected_behavior: Dynamically builds a parameterized UPDATE for only the provided fields, always sets updated_at=NOW() and updated_by from context user, and returns the updated row mapped to camelCase. If no row matches the id, throws GraphQLError NOT_FOUND. Other failures throw INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.

user_story: As an operator, I want to delete a connector configuration so that obsolete syncs are removed.
expected_behavior: Runs DELETE FROM connector_configurations WHERE id RETURNING id; if no row matched throws GraphQLError NOT_FOUND, otherwise returns {success:true, message:'Configuration deleted successfully'}. Other failures throw INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.

user_story: As an operator, I want to test a connector configuration's connection so that I can validate credentials before running it.
expected_behavior: Resolver-bound but a stub: always throws GraphQLError 'Not implemented yet' (NOT_IMPLEMENTED) with a TODO for ConnectorExecutor. No connection test is performed.
issue_note: testConnectorConnection is an unimplemented stub that always throws NOT_IMPLEMENTED (connector.resolvers.ts:1115-1118).

user_story: As an operator, I want to trigger a connector run so that I can sync data on demand.
expected_behavior: Resolver-bound but a stub: always throws GraphQLError 'Not implemented yet' (NOT_IMPLEMENTED) with a TODO for ConnectorExecutor. No run is enqueued.
issue_note: runConnector is an unimplemented stub that always throws NOT_IMPLEMENTED (connector.resolvers.ts:1128-1131).

user_story: As an operator, I want to cancel an in-progress connector run so that I can stop a stuck or unwanted sync.
expected_behavior: Resolver-bound but a stub: always throws GraphQLError 'Not implemented yet' (NOT_IMPLEMENTED) with a TODO for BullMQ job cancellation. No cancellation occurs.
issue_note: cancelConnectorRun is an unimplemented stub that always throws NOT_IMPLEMENTED (connector.resolvers.ts:1141-1144).

user_story: As an operator, I want to enable a connector configuration so that its scheduled syncs run.
expected_behavior: Runs UPDATE connector_configurations SET enabled=true, updated_at=NOW() WHERE id RETURNING *; throws GraphQLError NOT_FOUND if no row matched, otherwise returns the updated configuration mapped to camelCase. Other failures throw INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.

user_story: As an operator, I want to disable a connector configuration so that its syncs stop without deleting it.
expected_behavior: Runs UPDATE connector_configurations SET enabled=false, updated_at=NOW() WHERE id RETURNING *; throws GraphQLError NOT_FOUND if no row matched, otherwise returns the updated configuration. Other failures throw INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR.

user_story: As an analyst, I want an 'analytics' query namespace so that I can drill into CI/discovery/change analytics via its subfields.
expected_behavior: Schema defines extend type Query { analytics: AnalyticsQuery! } and a type AnalyticsQuery with the metric subfields. The Query.analytics resolver returns {} (subfields resolve on the AnalyticsQuery type). Defect: the AnalyticsQuery type resolver is exported under key '_AnalyticsQuery' (resolvers/index.ts:739) instead of 'AnalyticsQuery', so the type's field resolvers are unbound and every non-null subfield (PLAT-054..PLAT-066) resolves null/errors.
issue_note: AnalyticsQuery type resolver registered as _AnalyticsQuery (resolvers/index.ts:739) does not match schema type AnalyticsQuery (analytics.schema.ts:318); all analytics subfields are unwired.

user_story: As an analyst, I want CI counts grouped by type so that I can see the composition of the inventory.
expected_behavior: Calls AnalyticsService.getCICountsByType() and returns [{_ciType,_count}]; throws GraphQLError INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR on failure. The field resolver key matches the schema, but is only reachable if the AnalyticsQuery type binds (see PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want CI counts grouped by status so that I can monitor active vs decommissioned items.
expected_behavior: Calls AnalyticsService.getCICountsByStatus() and returns [{_status,_count}]; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want CI counts grouped by environment so that I can compare prod/staging/dev footprints.
expected_behavior: Calls AnalyticsService.getCICountsByEnvironment() and returns [{_environment,_count}]; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want relationship counts grouped by type so that I can understand how CIs are interconnected.
expected_behavior: Calls AnalyticsService.getRelationshipCounts() and returns [{_relationshipType,_count}]; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want discovery summary stats over a date range so that I can gauge how much was discovered and by which providers.
expected_behavior: Parses optional startDate/endDate, calls AnalyticsService.getDiscoveryStats(start,end), returns {_summary:{_totalCis,_uniqueTypes,_firstDiscovery,_lastDiscovery}, _byProvider:[{_discoveryProvider,_count}]}; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want a discovery timeline so that I can see how discovery volume changes over time.
expected_behavior: Converts interval (hour/day/week/month, default day) and clamps limit to max 365 (default 30), calls AnalyticsService.getDiscoveryTimeline(interval,limit), returns [{_period,_count,_uniqueTypes}]; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want the most connected CIs so that I can identify hubs and critical nodes.
expected_behavior: Clamps limit to max 100 (default 10), converts direction (in/out/both), calls AnalyticsService.getTopConnectedCIs(limit,direction), returns [{_ciId,_ciName,_ciType,_relationshipCount}]; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want dependency depth statistics so that I can spot deeply nested dependency chains.
expected_behavior: Calls AnalyticsService.getDependencyDepthStats() and returns {_topCis:[{_ciId,_maxDepth,_totalDependencies}], _depthDistribution:[{_maxDepth,_count}]}; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want a CI's change history so that I can audit what changed and when.
expected_behavior: Requires ciId (else BAD_USER_INPUT), clamps limit to max 1000 (default 50), calls AnalyticsService.getChangeHistory(ciId,limit), returns [{_changeTimestamp,_changeType,_fieldName,_oldValue,_newValue,_changedBy}]; re-throws GraphQLError, otherwise throws INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want change frequency grouped by CI type over time so that I can see which types churn most.
expected_behavior: Parses optional start/end dates and interval, calls AnalyticsService.getChangeFrequencyByType(start,end,interval), returns [{_ciType,_period,_changeCount}]; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As a dashboard, I want a single consolidated analytics summary so that I can render headline numbers and breakdowns in one call.
expected_behavior: Calls AnalyticsService.getDashboardStats() and returns {_summary:{_totalCis,_uniqueTypes,_uniqueEnvironments,_totalRelationships,_recentDiscoveries24h}, _breakdown:{_byType,_byStatus,_byEnvironment}}; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want time-series health metrics for a CI so that I can plot CPU/memory/disk and status changes.
expected_behavior: Requires ciId, startTime and endTime (else BAD_USER_INPUT), converts interval (5m/15m/1h/6h/1d), calls AnalyticsService.getCIHealthMetrics(ciId,start,end,interval), returns [{_timeBucket,_avgCpu,_avgMemory,_avgDisk,_statusChanges}] (nullable averages); re-throws GraphQLError, otherwise INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).

user_story: As an analyst, I want a filtered inventory report so that I can export CIs with their relationship counts.
expected_behavior: Maps an optional filter (_type/_status/_environment) to the service filter, calls AnalyticsService.getInventoryReport(filter), returns [{_ciId,_ciName,_ciType,_status,_environment,_discoveredAt,_relationshipCount}]; throws GraphQLError on failure. Reachable only if AnalyticsQuery binds (PLAT-053).
